message:
fix(scheduler): resolve three liveness bugs in work-stealing and shutdown paths

Fix three independent scheduler bugs that affect correctness and liveness:

1. local_queue: Steal starvation from local tasks blocking the queue tail
   A local (!Send) task sitting at the tail of a work queue would cause
   Stealer::steal() to push it back and immediately return None, blocking all
   stealable (Send) tasks behind it from ever being stolen. The steal loop now
   iterates through up to stack.len() entries, skipping local tasks with
   continue, so stealable tasks are found even when local tasks are interleaved.
   New test: steal_skips_local_tail_and_finds_remote

2. stealing: Arithmetic overflow in circular index computation
   The inline expression (start + i) % len can silently overflow usize when
   start and i are both large, producing an incorrect index. Extracted to a
   dedicated circular_index() helper using wrapping_add for correct modular
   arithmetic even at usize boundary values, with debug_assert on len > 0.
   New test: test_circular_index_wraps_without_overflow

3. worker: Shutdown hang when workers park indefinitely without unpark
   Workers in the backoff idle path called parker.park() indefinitely, requiring
   an explicit unpark() to wake. If shutdown was signaled while parked with no
   unpark, the worker would hang forever. Now checks shutdown flag at the top
   of each backoff iteration and uses park_timeout(1ms) instead of indefinite
   park, ensuring workers observe shutdown within milliseconds.
   New test: test_worker_shutdown_observed_without_explicit_unpark

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
